content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string SSS with length NNN. He also has MMM other strings P1,P2,…,PMP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_MP1​,P2​,…,PM​, which are called patterns. The characters in the strings are indexed from 111.

Chef was wondering if he could find the MMM patterns in the string SSS in the given order. That is, he wanted to know whether it is possible to choose MMM ranges, denoted by [li,ri][l_i, r_i][li​,ri​] for each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), such that 1≤l1≤r1<l2≤r2<…<lM≤rM≤N1 \le l_1 \le r_1 \lt l_2 \le r_2 \lt \ldots \lt l_M \le r_M \le N1≤l1​≤r1​<l2​≤r2​<…<lM​≤rM​≤N and for each valid iii, the substring Sli,Sli+1,…,SriS_{l_i}, S_{l_i + 1}, \ldots, S_{r_i}Sli​​,Sli​+1​,…,Sri​​ equals PiP_iPi​.

As this problem was too easy for Chef, he decided to make a harder one. A permutation p=(p1,p2,…,pM)p = (p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_M)p=(p1​,p2​,…,pM​) of integers 111 through MMM is called a matchable permutation if Chef can reorder the MMM patterns into Pp1,Pp2,…,PpMP_{p_1}, P_{p_2}, \ldots, P_{p_M}Pp1​​,Pp2​​,…,PpM​​ and then find them in SSS, in this order (in the same way as described above).

Can you help Chef find the number of matchable permutations?
constraints: S,P1,P2,…,PMS, P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_MS,P1​,P2​,…,PM​ contain only lowercase English letters

`Explanation 1Among the 3!=63! = 63!=6 possible permutations of (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), the matchable permutations are (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), (3,1,2)(3, 1, 2)(3,1,2) and (1,3,2)(1, 3, 2)(1,3,2). These correspond to permutations of patterns ("ab", "ac", "b"), ("b", "ab", "ac") and ("ab", "b", "ac") respectively. Each of them can be found in SSS in the given order.
`Explanation 2We cannot match the two patterns in such a way that their ranges do not intersect.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains a single string SSS.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤M)1 \le i \le M)1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines contains a single string PiP_iPi​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer - the number of matchable permutations.
